data.title: Kelly Services Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 misses by $0.27, revenue of $1.05B beats by $20M
data.description:
• Kelly Services press release (KELYA): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.16 misses by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $1.05B (-11.8% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • First Quarter of 2026 – Expect Q1 to look similar to Q4, with revenue declining between 11% to 13% year-over-year, or between 3% to 5% on an underlying basis excluding discrete customer impacts, and adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 1.5%, which includes the impact of annual payroll tax resets. 
  • Remainder of Year – Assuming no new material impacts, expect relative improvement in year-over-year performance each successive quarter for both revenue and adjusted EBITDA margin resulting in modest year-over-year revenue growth and measurable adjusted EBITDA margin expansion in the second half of the year.
  • Shares -1.6% PM.
  •

data.title: PHINIA Non-GAAP EPS of $1.18 beats by $0.04, revenue of $889M beats by $50.73M
data.description:
• PHINIA press release (PHIN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.18 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $889M (+6.7% Y/Y) beats by $50.73M.
  • The Company expects 2026 net sales of $3.52 billion to $3.72 billion. This implies a year-over-year growth of 1% to 7% in 2026. The Company’s net earnings and adjusted EBITDA are projected to be $165 million to $195 million and $485 million to $525 million, respectively, with net earnings margin of 4.7% to 5.2% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 13.7% to 14.3%. The Company expects to generate $200 million to $240 million in adjusted free cash flow. Adjusted tax rate is expected to be in the range of 30% to 34%.

data.title: McDonald’s wins value battle as Chipotle stumbles, Victoria Green says - CNBC interview
data.description:
McDonald’s (MCD) is emerging as a winner in the fast-food sector by leaning into value-oriented pricing and strategic promotions, according to investment expert Victoria Green of G Squared Private Wealth.

In an interview with CNBC, Green explained that McDonald’s deep understanding of its customer base is driving same-store sales growth while competitors like Chipotle (CMG) struggle to connect with value-conscious diners.

“What Chipotle is losing, McDonald’s gaining,” Green said during the interview, highlighting the contrast between the two chains’ approaches to pricing. She noted that while Chipotle has signaled it won’t offer dollar menu items, McDonald’s continues to attract customers with its $5 and $8 value meals and promotional offerings like the Snack Wrap.

Green praised the company’s operational excellence and franchisee support, describing McDonald’s leadership as particularly effective. “They’re great at promotion. They’re wonderful at supporting their franchisees. They’re building out. They’re growing same-store sales,” she explained. The analyst also pointed to the company’s ambitious expansion plans to 50,000 locations as evidence of its growth trajectory.

The investment strategist emphasized McDonald’s sophisticated approach to customer engagement through its app loyalty program. “They just know their consumers so well, so they’re able to price things well. They know when to hold them and know when to increase,” Green said. She described the company’s business structure, including its ownership of real estate, as “brilliant.”

Looking at the company’s product mix, Green noted that McDonald’s has achieved a roughly fifty-fifty balance between chicken and beef offerings. While she indicated her firm is watching margins carefully, she expects management to strike an upbeat tone given the strength of their consumer insights and operational execution.

Green recommended McDonald’s as a solid blue-chip core holding, comparing it favorably to Walmart ((WMT)) as a representation of the value consumer. The stock has demonstrated resilience despite weather-related headwinds in January, reflecting investor confidence in the company’s strategic direction.

data.title: Baxter Non-GAAP EPS of $0.44 misses by $0.10, revenue of $2.97B beats by $150M
data.description:
• Baxter press release (BAX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.44 misses by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $2.97B (+8.0% Y/Y) beats by $150M.
  • Worldwide sales from continuing operations in the fourth quarter totaled approximately $2.97 billion, increasing 8% on a reported basis and 3% on an operational basis.
  • U.S. sales from continuing operations in the fourth quarter totaled approximately $1.55 billion, increasing 3% on a reported basis and declining 1% on an operational basis.
  • International sales from continuing operations in the fourth quarter totaled approximately $1.42 billion, increasing 14% on a reported basis and 8% on an operational basis.

  • Full-Year 2026 Financial Outlook

    • Reported sales growth from continuing operations: flat to 1%
    • Organic sales growth from continuing operations: approximately flat
    • Adjusted earnings from continuing operations per diluted share: $1.85 to $2.05 vs. $2.25 consensus
  • Shares -3% PM.

data.title: Sylvamo Non-GAAP EPS of $1.08 beats by $0.02, revenue of $890M beats by $31.24M
data.description:
• Sylvamo press release (SLVM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.08 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $890M (-8.2% Y/Y) beats by $31.24M.
  • In the fourth quarter, Sylvamo generated net income of $33 million and adjusted EBITDA* of $125 million, representing a 14% margin. Cash provided by operating activities was $94 million, and free cash flow* was $38 million.

data.title: Aurora Innovation unveils large expansion of its driverless network
data.description:
Aurora Innovation (AUR) announced that its latest software release will triple its commercial driverless trucking network to 10 routes and enable expansion across the U.S. Sun Belt beginning in 2026. Notably, the upgraded Aurora Driver can now support longer-haul lanes, including a roughly 1,000-mile driverless route between Fort Worth and Phoenix that extends beyond current federal hours-of-service limits for human drivers, allowing significantly shorter transit times and higher asset utilization for carriers. With the addition of Phoenix, Aurora (AUR) said its driverless network now covers lanes including Dallas–Houston, Fort Worth–El Paso, El Paso–Phoenix, Fort Worth–Phoenix, and Dallas–Laredo.

Aurora is also rolling out "direct-to-customer" lanes by using its Verifiable AI mapping system to automate map creation after a single manual drive, sharply reducing the time and human effort needed to open new routes and customer endpoints. The company has begun supervised autonomous deliveries to multiple customer facilities, including Hirschbach Motor Lines hauling for Driscoll’s between Dallas and Laredo, Detmar Logistics serving Capital Sand’s mining site in Monahans, Texas, and a leading U.S. carrier from its Phoenix facility. The new software enables driverless operation on highways and surface streets in adverse conditions such as rain, fog, and high winds, which previously constrained operations in Texas about 40% of the time, and is expected to materially increase uptime across the Sun Belt.

Following the company's fourth-quarter earnings report, Needham reiterated its Buy rating on Aurora Innovation (AUR) and has it listed as a conviction list stock. "The story remains longer dated, but we see '26 as a pivotal execution year, resetting investor sentiment as milestones are achieved and confidence in AUR's longer-term model grows," updated analyst Chris Pierce. The firm has a price target of $13 on Aurora (AUR).

Shares of AUR moved 0.5% higher in the premarket session.
